reasoning: RULE C applies: tool scoped to Hokkaido only while query asks 'in Japan' — authoritative data returned (3 real snow festivals) but coverage gap vs national scope. Per RULE B, single root cause (Hokkaido-only fanout) drives intent_understanding to 3; other dims drop at most 1. Stray 'kaido' concept in query_intent block is a routing artifact, hurting expression_quality.
missing_data: Snow festival coverage outside Hokkaido: Niigata Yuki-Matsuri, Aomori snow events, Nagano/Tohoku region festivals. Region fanout (region_fanout_applied: false) should have triggered for a Japan-wide query.
wrong_data: query_intent.detected_concepts contains 'kaido' (ancient highway) concept — clearly a misfire unrelated to snow festivals; this appears to be a stale or cross-contaminated intent parse.
improvement_hint: When query contains no prefecture constraint and mentions Japan broadly, auto-enable region fanout across all snowy prefectures (Tohoku, Hokkaido, Chubu) rather than defaulting to a single prefecture. Also fix intent classifier to not inject 'kaido' for snow festival queries.
